item.title: 'The need is constant': Red Cross calls for more blood donors
item.description: For families relying on transfusions, donated blood can mean the difference between life and death. For others, the decision to donate is shaped by culture, experience and how well they understand the system. Lifeblood is urging more Australians to donate blood, warning the need is constant and cannot be met without regular donors. But as Australia's population becomes more diverse, experts say the country's blood supply also needs to reflect that change, with some patients needing blood that is more closely matched to their background.

item.title: As voter disillusionment grows, why aren't voters flocking to the Greens in Australia?
item.description: Growing voter dissatisfaction with mainstream politics has led to a rise in popularity for One Nation. The same cannot be said for the Greens.

item.title: Australia 'relies on immigration' and restricting numbers could backfire, government warns
item.description: Dramatic decreases in migrant numbers will make the situation worse, the federal government says.

item.title: These Australians chose to be child-free. They want more spaces that don't allow kids
item.description: With the fertility rate at a record low and more Australians choosing not to have kids, some want more child-free spaces.

item.title: Why the safest path to a high salary may no longer be a degree
item.description: The advice for many has long been "get a degree to earn more" — but the labour market is also rewarding vocational skills.

item.title: People tell me I’ll regret not having kids. Why I know they're wrong
item.description: Jess chose to be child-free and had her fallopian tubes removed. She says she feels dismissed when people say she'll regret it.

item.title: The US-Iran peace deal may be Donald Trump's gain — and Benjamin Netanyahu's problem
item.description: Trump's calling it a win. Netanyahu's under mounting pressure. Experts say it might only get the US back to square one.

item.title: Government invests in social enterprise program for migrants
Israeli ambassador says AFP flotilla investigation 'wasting time'
item.description: A group of Australian activists have accused the Israeli military of sexual assault and abuse.

item.title: Travel restrictions eased for Australians travelling to some parts of Middle East
item.description: While restrictions have been eased in some areas, Foreign Minister Penny Wong has warned against non-essential travel.

item.title: Hanson casts One Nation as rising anti-establishment force in protest-hit Press Club speech
item.description: The One Nation leader said she would seek to ban immigrants from countries that fostered "radical Islam".

item.title: 'Be more responsible': Trump makes fresh jab at Netanyahu amid signs of growing rift
item.description: Once Israel's "greatest friend" in the White House, could Donald Trump's latest comments signal trouble for Israel and the US?

item.title: Who is Socceroos coach Tony Popovic? The story of Australia's World Cup history-maker
item.description: A former colleague has revealed the demanding fitness and diet standards imposed by Socceroos coach Tony Popovic.

item.title: After buying, Carey started feeling regret. Nearly half of first home buyers feel the same
item.description: What happens when the property-buying dream becomes tinged with regret?

item.title: The US and Iran have published their official agreement. Here's what's in it
item.description: Here is everything you need to know about the 14-point agreement between the US and Iran.

item.title: How will the capital gain tax reforms work after the government's carve-outs?
item.description: Around 2.7 million small businesses will be offered an exemption from the proposed capital gains tax reforms, amid backlash.

item.title: Government unveils capital gains tax carve-outs | Midday News Bulletin 18 June 2026
item.description: The government announces capital gains tax changes for small business... a Vodafone outage affects millions of Australians... and, at the World Cup, England and Ghana kick off their campaigns with wins

item.title: Pauline Hanson reveals One Nation policies at the NPC
item.description: Pauline Hanson has declared Australia should be a monoculture, in a sweeping address at the nation's most coveted political platform, the National Press Club. She's also criticised the public broadcasters and confirmed its official One Nation policy to scrap funding to SBS.

item.title: G7 "turning point" as world powers back Ukraine
item.description: After three days of meetings with world leaders at the G-7 summit in France, Ukraine has secured a unanimous pledge for more support in its war with Russia. French President Emmanuel Macron has hailed the meeting as a moment of unity after months of disagreements, saying Russia has shown no desire to end the years-long conflict.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 18 June 2026
item.description: A short news bulletin for those learning English and those with disability

item.title: Hanson says one of Australia’s biggest ideas has 'failed'. The reality is more complex
item.description: A less diverse Australia might not benefit the economy as some expect, experts suggest.

item.title: US-Iran strike a deal to make a deal, Bibi and Trump’s growing rift & could you be Japan’s next emperor?
item.description: US-Iran strike a deal to make a deal, Bibi and Trump’s growing rift & could you be Japan’s next emperor? Tehran and Trump both sound triumphant but who is the real winner so far? And will Israel’s refusal to leave Lebanon upend negotiations? Plus, fresh from hosting cage fights at the White House, Donald Trump joins G7 leaders to pressure Putin, while players become political footballs at the FIFA World Cup, and Japan has a royal dilemma.

item.title: US points to higher interest rates as markets fall
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Equity Trustees Chief Investment Officer Darren Thompson about the next move in US interest rates after the Federal Reserve left them on hold, along with his outlook for the market.

item.title: As the Socceroos shine on the world stage, a different victory is unfolding at home
item.description: Migrant advocates say the Socceroos' diversity sends a message that they belong and is a counterpoint to anti-migrant sentiment.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 19 June 2026
item.description: A 5-minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Which parties want to cut migration, and how far would they go?
item.description: Migration is back in the spotlight. Here's how the major parties' policies compare.

item.title: BHP suffers biggest slide in more than a year
item.description: The Australian sharemarket fell sharply on Friday as a 5.6 per cent plunge in BHP shares weighed heavily on the ASX 200, offsetting gains from CSL, energy stocks and the major supermarkets. BHP's decline followed a cost blowout at its Canadian potash project, although the mining giant's shares remain up around 70 per cent over the past year. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Andrew Wielandt, Partner at DP Wealth Advisory, about the day's market moves and what's driving investor sentiment.

item.title: One election, one question: Is Netanyahu finally running out of time?
item.description: In a pivotal election, Israel faces a stark choice between Benjamin Netanyahu's camp and those determined to replace him.

item.title: The good news for petrol prices — and the catch for Australian households
item.description: Petrol prices hit four-month lows, but a fuel discount is ending. Plus: Borrowers under pressure, and an Aussie giant's big slide.

item.title: As Australia celebrates one million refugees, some employers are stepping up offering jobs and training
item.description: This week, Australia celebrates the one million refugees who’ve made a home here since the end of World War Two. While some employers are offering new jobs and training, there are also calls to cut the refugee intake and some initiatives are at risk.

item.title: A crucial election is coming in Israel: Can Benjamin Netanyahu hold on to power?
item.description: A crucial election is looming for Israel and its prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu. It's shaping up to be less a fight between right and left than a contest between Netanyahu's supporters and rivals. The initial peace deal between the U-S and Iran has seen angry reactions across Israel, with many angry at Netanyahu for what his opponents say is a 'shocking failure'. So, where does the nation's longest-serving prime minister stand as the vote approaches?

item.title: Haiti and Türkiye out of the World Cup, Morocco overcomes Scotland
item.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón was sent off for covering his mouth and the USA became the first team in World Cup history to benefit from own-goals in consecutive games.

item.title: Haiti and Türkiye out of the World Cup, Morocco overcomes Scotland
item.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón was sent off for covering his mouth and the USA became the first team in World Cup history to benefit from own-goals in consecutive games.

item.title: Haiti and Türkiye out of the World Cup, Morocco overcomes Scotland
item.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón was sent off for covering his mouth and the USA became the first team in World Cup history to benefit from own-goals in consecutive games.

item.title: The 'secret sauce' behind the tiny town that's cracked Australia's energy problem
item.description: This town isn't waiting for the energy transition — it's powering it.

item.title: 'This world of darkness': An Australian shares his experience of living in Ukraine
item.description: Australian journalist Francis Farrell is living in Ukraine to document the human cost of war. He says the reality for many is grim.

item.title: A spike in drug overdose deaths sparks calls for more action
item.description: Australia is losing one person to a drug overdose every three and a half hours. New figures from the Penington Institute show more than 2,500 Australians died from drug-induced causes in a single year, the deadliest year on record. CEO John Ryan says the toll is now almost double the national road toll, and frontline workers warn the illicit drug market is becoming more unpredictable, with addiction affecting people from all walks of life.

item.title: Adapt or Perish: Why renewables could be a gold rush for regional Australia
item.description: This is part two of a two part series on how a small town in Victoria has become resilient through going electric. This episode, SBS looks at how the project has evolved and how other regional communities can stand to benefit from the rollout of renewables.

item.title: 'I had no idea': The tool at the centre of this family's aged care nightmare
item.description: The federal government has quietly launched a rapid review into a controversial algorithm after older Australians sound the alarm.

item.title: Justified or FIFA PR? Why footballers can't cover their mouths at the World Cup
item.description: A controversial World Cup-first unfolded during the Paraguay-Türkiye clash, with some opinions divided.

item.title: UN warns of 'imminent risk of mass atrocities' in Sudan
item.description: The Security Council said aid workers were preparing for the movement of large numbers of people from an area in the country's south.

item.title: Army veteran Jules went blind in one eye. Then he became a millionaire
item.description: A windfall can bring stability and a sense of contentment for some. For others, it is a more complex experience, exposing vulnerabilities.

item.title: 'Now we're on a map': Meet one of Australia's loneliest World Cup fans
item.description: There are only 40 people from this country in Australia — one of them tells SBS News how it feels to see their team shine in the World Cup.

item.title: Authorities declare readiness as H5N1 bird flu reaches Australia
item.description: Officials have confirmed the first case of the H5 avian influenza strain on the Australian mainland. The virus was identified in a wild migratory seabird who died after being found sick on a remote beach in Esperance, south of Perth. Authorities in Australia say they have been expecting the arrival of the virus and have been preparing accordingly.

item.title: Europe prepares for extreme heat as France issues red alerts
item.description: Europe is bracing for more extreme heat as temperatures climb and authorities impose restrictions.

item.title: Police have found 2.7 tonnes of cocaine in western Sydney | SBS News in Easy English 22 June 2026
item.description: A 5-minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: As second H5 bird flu case detected, Australia puts years of planning into action
item.description: The H5N1 strain has been confirmed in a northern giant petrel, near where the virus was first detected in Western Australia.

item.title: Labor facing fight to push tax, NDIS reforms through parliament
item.description: Federal Parliament has returned for two final sitting weeks before a five-week long winter recess. Twin fights over proposed tax reform and an overhaul to the National Disability Insurance Scheme are set to dominate debate in the coming days.

item.title: Survivors of the Stolen Generations use art to reclaim history
item.description: It is a history that was buried for many years. Now the stories of Stolen Generations are being told through artworks in a new exhibition. Warning: this story contains content listeners may find distressing.

item.title: This Socceroo has spent little time on the pitch this World Cup. He's still making an impact
item.description: The midfielder isn't afraid to mix sports with politics, putting the spotlight on some of football's most pervasive problems.

item.title: What property buyers and sellers need to know as auction market cools
item.description: Australia’s sharemarket edged lower as investors weighed a softer property market and fresh company news. Cotality Research Director Tim Lawless joins the podcast to explain why auction clearance rates have fallen to their lowest level since April 2020, what rising withdrawals mean for the housing market, and the opportunities and risks facing buyers and sellers. Then, Mark Gardner, CEO of MPC Markets, breaks down the day on the ASX, including the sharp sell-off in Wisetech, weakness among miners, gains for the banks, and what he is telling clients as markets search for direction.

item.title: The new financial year is here — and so are major changes. Here's what to know
item.description: From pay rises and tax changes to payday super, parental leave and free electricity, here are the major changes now in effect.

item.title: Emotional Starmer quits as Burnham favoured to be next UK PM
item.description: British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the Labour Party. The move follows months of mounting pressure from within his party, and deteriorating public support.

item.title: Iran denies US nuclear inspection claim | Easy English 23 June 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: 'They'll walk away': Home-sellers change tack as auction clearance rates plunge
item.description: Auctions aren't completely disappearing, but softer conditions are changing when some sellers decide to make a deal.

item.title: Albanese red cards monoculture claims, says Socceroos examples of 'rich' Australian culture
item.description: Anthony Albanese's comments came as Pauline Hanson walked back previous comments about wanting an end to parental leave.

item.title: Support for multiculturalism is declining. Why is that so?
item.description: A new report by the Lowy Institute reveals support for multiculturalism in Australia has plunged in the past two years. A recent poll has linked a decline in support for both current migration levels and multiculturalism, which coincides with growing fears about economic and social safety.

item.title: An egg shortage? Human infection? The ways H5 bird flu could impact Australia
item.description: The H5 bird flu variant can infect poultry, domestic cats, dairy cattle and a range of wildlife.

item.title: Why you should wait to lodge your tax return as SMSF rules change
item.description: With tax time approaching, ATO Assistant Commissioner Anita Challen explains why Australians should avoid lodging returns too early and how waiting for pre-filled information could help prevent delays and costly mistakes. Plus Stockspot CEO Chris Brycki speaks with Ricardo Gonçalves about how the latest changes to SMSFs may influence the sharemarket.

item.title: Has Trump and Netanyahu's push for regime change in Iran backfired?
item.description: Far from abolishing or weakening it, some believe the US-Israeli war has left Iran's regime emboldened and strengthened.

item.title: $400k, $630k or $1m? How to know which super number matters to you
item.description: There are a lot of retirement figures and estimates floating around. What should you actually aim for?

item.title: 'Desperate' IVF patients offered treatments with no proven fertility benefits
item.description: Many IVF patients would pay for costly extra treatments to increase chances of pregnancy, but new research find many aren't effective.

item.title: Treasurer says fuel excise 'not the whole story' amid underlying inflation rise
item.description: Jim Chalmers said the costs and consequences of the war in the Middle East "will be felt for some time".

item.title: Are energy prices going up? l SBS News in Easy English 24 June 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: World Cup: Ronaldo silences critics with a double; England stumble in goalless draw
item.description: Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo silences his critics with a double goal at the FIFA World Cup 2026™, giving their side a 5-nil win against Uzbekistan. Harry Kane misses chances in England's nil-nil match against Ghana. Croatia's Ante Budimir makes a massive impact off the bench - leading Croatia 1-nil against Panama after half time. A late goal by Daniel Munoz lifts Colombia 1-nil against DR Congo. FIFA boss Gianni Infantino pushes back against the controversies around hydration breaks. And Socceroos' Mat Leckie set to miss game against Paraguay. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she discusses if Portugal's Ronaldo has a chance to win the golden boot; and brings you the latest talking points on the hydration break controversy.

item.title: The US Senate passes a war powers bill to block military action against Iran
item.description: The US Senate has passed a war powers bill to block US military action against Iran. It comes as Tehran and Washington disagree over Iran's commitment to nuclear inspections, and the UN begins an evacuation of stranded seafarers through the Strait of Hormuz. A UN inquiry has also found Israeli authorities and security forces have deliberately targeted Palestinian children in Gaza and the West Bank.

item.title: South Australia has confirmed its first case of H5 bird flu as outbreak crosses state lines
item.description: CSIRO testing has confirmed that a migratory seabird found on the Fleurieu Peninsula had the deadly disease.

item.title: Inflation cools but rate rise risks remain
item.description: Australia's inflation rate eased in May, offering some relief to households, but sticky underlying price pressures suggest the Reserve Bank may not be finished with interest rates just yet

item.title: What you won't get this tax time — and the claims the ATO will be watching
item.description: Australian taxpayers will have to wait until next year for a proposed deduction to come into effect.

item.title: France records hottest day on record as Europe swelters through brutal heatwave
item.description: An estimated 44 million people in France are currently under the highest red alert level for heat.

item.title: Third bird flu result recorded
UN slams Australia over offshore detention
Final 'ISIS bride' granted permit to return to Australia, Tony Burke says
item.description: The woman had been barred from the country due to national security concerns, but will now be allowed to return with her child.

item.title: A deleted interview, TV fallout, and Hanson's job offer: Karl Stefanovic's turbulent 48 hours
item.description: His controversial interview with far-right activist Tommy Robinson has been taken offline, but the Karl Stefanovic show goes on.

item.title: Taylor bats away multiculturalism saga criticism, says Australia shouldn’t 'look like Japan'
item.description: Angus Taylor has sought to clarify the Coalition's stance on multiculturalism, after Pauline Hanson called for a "monocultural" society.

item.title: Earthquakes Hit Venezuela | SBS News in Easy English 25 June 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Deadly bird flu strain lands in second state – but experts say the risk of pandemic remains low
item.description: The deadly H5N1 bird flu has reached a second Australian state, with a third seabird confirmed infected and more cases expected to grow on the mainland. But authorities say the risk of a human pandemic remains low.

item.title: ASIO outlines looming security threats on and beyond Australian soil
item.description: ASIO has revealed it has foiled 14 terror plots since the December mass shooting in Bondi, with the current terror level remaining "probable". But the service will undergo a review of its warning system, as Australia's spy chief warns the current one is understating the danger faced by the public.

item.title: Everything you need to know about Labor's latest tax changes
item.description: The sweeping changes to Australia's economy passed federal parliament today, following a deal between Labor and the Greens.

item.title: Unemployment rate falls as oil, gold and Australian dollar hit milestones
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate unexpectedly eased in May despite a cooling labour market, but what does it mean for the Reserve Bank after inflation also ticked higher? Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Indeed APAC Senior Economist Callam Pickering about the outlook for interest rates, before LGT Wealth Management Chief Investment Officer Scott Haslem explains why investors moved into defensive stocks as the Australian dollar slipped below 69 US cents, oil fell below pre-conflict levels and gold dropped under US$4,000.

item.title: Are buyers like Mehdi the winners of Australia’s housing downturn?
item.description: Australia’s housing market is shifting, creating new opportunities for some and fresh uncertainty for others.

item.title: Sickies, long lunches, green-and-gold fever: Australia set for a midday Socceroos party
item.description: With the Socceroos one match away from the World Cup knockout rounds, employers are being urged to embrace football fever.

item.title: The 'yellow line' Gazans have been killed for crossing, according to new report
item.description: Israel rejected the UN's independent inquiry, which found children in Gaza are still targeted after the ceasefire.

item.title: Trump touts progress with Iran, Starmer resigns & the far-right on the rise in Australia
item.description: Trump and JD Vance insist talks for a final deal between the US and Iran are progressing smoothly, but does Iran agree? Keir Starmer bows to party pressure and resigns as PM, what's next for the UK? Plus, SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson talks One Nation's rise up the polls in Australia, and her run-in with leader Pauline Hanson. And, it's fans, fun and fishy business during this week's action at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: King Charles is the first UK monarch to release their tax bill. Here's what it shows
item.description: It was also revealed that the king and Queen Camilla will not live in Buckingham Palace after a £369 million refurbishment.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | 26 June 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Tech sell-off rocks Asia as investors contemplate outlook
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished higher despite a volatile session and a global technology sell-off that triggered a second circuit breaker in South Korea this week. Apple price hikes and concerns over AI valuations weighed on tech stocks, while gold miners found support and the ASX closed the week down 0.9 per cent. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Tom Wickenden from Betashares about what drove today's market moves and what investors should watch next.

item.title: The night sky is fading. What happens when we lose the stars?
item.description: As the night sky grows brighter, some Australians are fighting to save what's disappearing from above them.

item.title: Chinese food in Australia is changing – thanks to a new generation of chefs
item.description: Across Australia, rising costs and staff shortages have forced many traditional Chinese restaurants to close. Some younger chefs are forging a new path with fusion styles that celebrate local ingredients.

item.title: How scientists are using surrogates to protect Australia's most endangered wallaby
item.description: Everyone from rugby union supporters to wildlife and animal lovers has heard of the Australian wallaby. But it's perhaps less known that there are, in fact, many species of these cute marsupials, including some that are endangered. One of these is the brush-tailed rock-wallaby and scientists are now working on ways to protect the species.

item.title: Tax time nears, inflation eases — and property loses momentum
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply — and what they mean for you.

item.title: Why Chinese restaurants are disappearing — and evolving
item.description: As costs rise and tastes shift, a new generation is redefining Chinese food in Australia.

item.title: Socceroos stop the nation as Australia v Paraguay smashes World Cup viewing records
item.description: Were you one of the 4.8 million who tuned in on Friday?

item.title: 'This is history': World Cup fairytale performance thrills small nation's local diaspora
item.description: The nation of just 500,000 people has defied the odds to reach the knockout rounds, delighting Cape Verdeans in Australia.

item.title: Fourth positive bird flu case confirmed amid surge of calls to animal disease hotline
item.description: More than four hundred reports have been made to the emergency animal hotline in just over a week.

item.title: 'Thinfluencers': For Luka, healthy eating became an obsession. Her mum blames content creators
item.description: A new generation of influencers is promoting extreme thinness to millions online — with often-tragic consequences.

item.title: High-speed rail is a decades-old Australian dream. One factor could finally make it real
item.description: It's been debated for decades, but some experts say conditions may finally be right for high-speed rail in Australia.

item.title: 6.7 million people affected by Venezuela earthquakes, UN says, locals plead for more aid
item.description: The death toll for the disaster that struck the South American nation on Thursday has risen to more than 14,000.

item.title: Iran again asserts control over Strait of Hormuz after Trump's Truth Social annihilation threat
item.description: The Iranian foreign minister has asserted Iran's control of the strategic waterway after a dire warning from the US president.

item.title: From Cape Verde to drinks breaks: The World Cup group stage's surprises and stinkers
item.description: From Cape Verde to Uruguay — and from Messi to Ronaldo — the 2026 World Cup has had its share of surprises and disappointments.

item.title: History for DR Congo, heartbreak for Iran and Round of 32 set
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026™ Round of 32 is set. England tops their group after two goals by Jude Bellingham and the Three Lions’ captain Harry Kane against Panama. Croatia are also through to the knockout stage after a 2-1 win over Ghana. Colombia and Portugal played out a nil-nil draw. DR Congo reaches the elimination phase for the first time, after emerging triumphant over Uzbekistan 3-1. It's their first ever World Cup victory - 52 years after their only previous appearance at the tournament. Algeria drew 3-all against Austria in a dramatic finish. Algeria’s captain Riyad Mahrez produced a moment of brilliance in the 94th minute - scoring a double in the match. Not long after, Austria's Sasa Kalajdzic equalised. Argentina delivered another statement performance with a comfortable 3-1 win over Jordan, Lionel Messi came off the bench to score the third goal. Australia now turns its attention to Egypt in Dallas next Saturday. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she looks into the DR Congo’s historic comeback. Does Austria and Algeria's dramatic match affect Iran? And, will the Egyptian king be fit to face Australia?

item.title: Alex and Judy have been together for 30 years, but they've never shared a home
item.description: For some Australians who meet their partner later in life, the concept of a 'living apart together' relationship can be appealing.

item.title: How good are Egypt? Meet Australia's World Cup opponent ahead of an 'almighty' match
item.description: One of football's best-known supercomputers predicts an extremely close contest between Australia and Egypt.

item.title: Those in Australia feel the shift. Those outside are watching closely
item.description: One Nation's rise is hardening Australia's migration debate — shaping belonging for those inside and decisions for those outside.

item.title: Watch: Stoppage-time goal lifts co-hosts Canada to last 16 at World Cup
item.description: Co-hosts Canada and South Africa both made World Cup history as they met in the opening game of the knockout round.

item.title: Paul Hogan hits back at 'pelican' Hanson over 'monoculture' claim
item.description: The One Nation leader's approval ratings dropped after her National Press Club speech.

item.title: Some households can get free electricity from Wednesday. Are you eligible?
item.description: A new scheme will give some households three hours of free electricity each day — even if they don't have rooftop solar.

item.title: Renewed fighting tests fragile peace deal for US, Iran
item.description: Just two weeks after the United States and Iran agreed to halt four months of fighting, renewed military exchanges are testing the fragile ceasefire. As tensions rise around the Strait of Hormuz, violence continues in Lebanon and Gaza, raising fresh questions about the prospects for lasting peace across the Middle East.

item.title: Government warns against AI content | SBS News in Easy English 29 June 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: The $54 million sponsorships raising questions about influence over Aussie kids
item.description: Six children's programs had a $54 million backing from resource companies, with hundreds more deals, prompting calls for scrutiny.

item.title: Australians caught up in Venezuela quake as Gold Coast firefighter tells of survivor search
item.description: With no Spanish or local contacts, Craig De Meillon flew to Venezuela to help search for earthquake survivors.

item.title: 1300 heat-linked deaths: why experts say Europe is in danger when it comes to extreme heat
item.description: The World Health Organisation says more than 1300 deaths can now be linked to Europe's unprecedented summer heatwave. As the heat moves east, all-time temperature records have been broken in Germany, Poland and the Czech Republic. Experts say with global warming making heatwaves more frequent, severe, and deadly, Europe is particularly vulnerable when it comes to soaring temperatures.

item.title: Super Platforms Face ASIC Scrutiny as the ASX Rallies Into Financial Year End
item.description: The ASX 200 rallied 0.7 per cent in a late-session surge ahead of the final trading day of the financial year, with tech stocks leading the gains. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with ASIC Commissioner Simone Constant about why the corporate watchdog says superannuation trustees must do more to protect members from high fees and risky investments on investment platforms. Plus, Kyle Rodda from Capital.com breaks down the day's market action and what investors can expect as the financial year draws to a close.

item.title: Australia has signed a long-awaited Vanuatu security deal. Here's what's in it
item.description: After 10 months, the lucrative deal between Australia and Vanuatu has been signed. What does it mean for the Pacific?

item.title: Police reveal final messages of Thai teenager before she was allegedly killed by Australian
item.description: Police colonel Anek Srathongyoo has given detailed information on the case of an Australian man charged with murder in Thailand.

item.title: Australians are feeling the strain of financial pressure. Could 'frugal influencers' help?
item.description: The influencer business model has long been based on selling a luxury lifestyle. But these creators want to do the opposite.

item.title: Watch: Morocco bring the Netherlands undone to close day of penalty shootout upsets
item.description: Just one rank apart in the world rankings, Netherlands v Morocco was a box-office match in the making. It didn't disappoint.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 1,700 as public anger over government response grows
item.description: As rescuers continue to search for survivors, frustration over the Venezuelan government's sluggish response to the disaster is growing.

item.title: 'Deliberate' explosion rocks Monaco, Ukrainian oligarch injured
item.description: Monaco's minister of state has said the explosion is the first such event ever to take place in the principality.

item.title: Australia heralds deal with Vanuatu as a 'transformational breakthrough' in the Pacific
item.description: Australia and Vanuatu have signed a 500 million dollar strategic agreement - the first ever inked between the two countries. It positions Australia as the Pacific country's security partner of choice, but it's a weaker treaty than the Australian prime minister originally hoped for. China has expressed concern the deal targets China, warning Australia against playing "geopolitical games".

item.title: China reacts to Australia’s deal with Vanuatu | SBS News in Easy English 30 June 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: The country that's been dragged in to Australia's 'monoculture' debate
item.description: Pauline Hanson has held up Japan as a model of monoculturalism. Experts say the reality is more complex.

item.title: Could this be Australia’s warmest winter ever?
item.description: The ski season has started, but the weather might mean it isn't worthwhile hitting the slopes just yet.

item.title: Heartbreak for Japan; Brazil, Paraguay and Morocco through; Netherlands and Germany out in penalties
item.description: Netherlands out of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ after Morocco’s penalty shootout victory. Paraguay also shocked Germany in a penalty shootout upset. Heartbreak for Japan, as Brazil through to the Round of 16 with 2-1 win. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she breaks down today’s penalty upsets. Did Brazil break Japan's World Cup dream? How did Paraguay pull off one of the biggest shocks of the tournament? Is Netherlands through to the round of 16? How are the Socceroos preparing to face Egypt?

item.title: ASX lags global markets this financial year but super delivers strong returns
item.description: The Australian sharemarket ended the financial year with a modest gain, trailing the strong performance of global markets, but many Australians are still set to see healthy returns in their super. SBS On the Money speaks with Kai Chen, Head of Investment at MPC Markets, about why the ASX underperformed overseas markets, and Jeff Brunton, Deputy Chief Investment Officer at HESTA, on why diversified super funds are expected to deliver solid returns and what APRA's latest report says about the growing influence of Australia's superannuation sector on the nation's financial stability.

item.title: The city defying Australia's housing slump — and why sellers are still reaping record gains
item.description: Despite fresh price falls, many homeowners are still selling well ahead.

item.title: Pay less, owe it longer: What the HELP shake-up means for young Australians
item.description: New HELP rules mean smaller repayments now — but a longer debt later. We crunch the numbers on who really comes out ahead.

item.title: Australia's supermarket price crackdown starts today. But will you pay less?
item.description: The new laws target excessive grocery prices, but experts say their impact at the checkout may be limited.

item.title: WATCH: Mexico dismantle Ecuador with pitch perfect play and a pair of beautiful goals
item.description: Meanwhile, Mbappé got a double for France, and Norway celebrated a historic win over Côte d'Ivoire.

item.title: Police hunt for Monaco bomb suspect after Ukrainian tycoon wounded
item.description: Home to some of the world's wealthiest people, the incident has rocked the ultra-safe principality.

item.title: 'She has such an aura': The 20-year-old Aussie who just beat Serena Williams at Wimbledon
item.description: Rising Australian tennis star Maya Joint produced the best match of her career to prevail over the tennis legend.

item.title: Australia's happiness divide has been revealed — and one worry stands out
item.description: More than 15,000 Australians were asked about happiness, optimism and the nation’s biggest concerns. One issue dominated.

item.title: Fuel prices rise as discount ends | SBS News in Easy English 1 July 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: More than 12,000km from Washington, Trump's major move is still being felt
item.description: One year on from the dismantling of the world's largest donor of humanitarian aid, the impacts are devastating.

item.title: Royal Commission hears X is amplifying antisemitic content | Evening News Bulletin 1 July 2026
item.description: Royal Commission hears the social media platform X is amplifying antisemitic content; crossbench MPs back a call for the government to implement online gambling recommendations; Mexico beats Ecuador 2-nil to go through to the Round of 16.

item.title: World Cup: Mexico through; Mbappé record; Haaland shines
item.description: Norway is through to the Round of 16 at the FIFA World Cup 2026™ after a hard-fought 2-1 win against Ivory Coast. Manchester City striker Erling Haaland scores the team's second goal. France is also through, after a commanding 3-nil win over Sweden. Kylian Mbappé scored a double, World Cup goal tally moves to 18 just behind Lionel Messi by one goal. Mexico celebrates first knockout win since 1986 with Julián Quiñones and Raúl Jiménez strike in first half, scoring 2-nil against Ecuador. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she breaks down the latest results from today’s matches, and brings you the latest on the Socceroos’ preparations against Egypt.

item.title: How 120,000 siblings disproved a myth around pregnancy and paracetamol
item.description: A new study found paracetamol use in pregnant mothers does not increase the risk of autism or ADHD.

item.title: Home prices record biggest monthly fall in three years as shares start the new financial year weaker
item.description: The new financial year has begun with a weaker tone for investors, as the ASX 200 fell 0.6 per cent and Australia's housing market recorded its biggest monthly decline since June 2022. SBS On the Money examines what is driving the latest property data with Tim Lawless, Head of Research at Cotality, before unpacking the day's market moves, sector rotation and takeover speculation with Hebe Chen, Market Analyst at Vantage Australia.

item.title: Ninth life: What 30 years of housing downturns can tell us about this one
item.description: Yes, property prices are falling. Here's what happened the last eight times — and the signs worth watching now.

item.title: Who had it worse? The hidden shift behind Australia’s generational mortgage debate
item.description: New research suggests borrowers are under greater stress than ever — including 1989 when interest rates were 17.5 per cent.

item.title: Cecily always wanted to marry Terry. She had to wait until he turned 90
item.description: Terry, 90, and Cecily, 89, have been together nearly 40 years. They say they're very different, but love each other more as time goes on.

item.title: WATCH: Folarin Balogun draws controversial red card as the USA runs Bosnia and Herzegovina ragged
item.description: Elsewhere, it was a day of nervous fight-backs with England and Belgium both coming from behind to triumph at the death.

item.title: Man arrested for people smuggling in QLD | Morning News Bulletin 2 July 2026
item.description: A man arrested on people smuggling charges after a boat arrival in far north Queensland; Iran and the US wrap their latest negotiations; Tragedy strikes celebrations in Mexico after their knockout victory against Ecuador.

item.title: 'Damning admission': Labor concession on controversial aged care tool fails to quell anger
item.description: Critics say a tool designed to streamline aged care assessments is leaving some people waiting too long.

item.title: Survivors plea for more support after deadly Venezuela quakes | Midday News Bulletin 2 July 2026
item.description: Two arrested over the stabbing death of a teenager in north Melbourne; Locals call for more support in post earthquake search and rescue missions in Venezuela; Belgium shocks with a last minute goal to beat Senegal.

item.title: What's behind South Africa's deadly anti-immigrant protests?
item.description: Organisers claim they are protesting against undocumented migrants, but some are seeking to spread the blame for other issues

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | Fans in Mexico die after football match
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Gun buyback deadline expires with most states still refusing to sign on
item.description: A federal government spokesperson indicated the scheme would still go ahead, citing the agreement of National Cabinet.

item.title: A week after Venezuela's quakes, one missed call is keeping hope alive
item.description: Families are clinging to signs of life beneath the rubble as anger grows over Venezuela's earthquake response.

item.title: Jewish Council appears at Royal Commission | Evening News Bulletin 2 July 2026
item.description: Jewish Council of Australia appears at the Royal Commission; strong criticism of the government's gambling reforms; pubs set to stay open for Saturday morning's World Cup.

item.title: UN panel releases first report laying out benefits - and risks - of artificial intelligence
item.description: A major United Nations scientific panel has released its first global assessment on artificial intelligence. Its report has found artificial intelligence is advancing faster than governments and regulators can keep up. The experts also warn the technology poses growing risks - including cyber threats, disinformation and potentially catastrophic harm.

item.title: Venezuela twin quake catastrophe, not-so-Strait shooting hinders US-Iran deal & Cuba opens up
item.description: Grief and anger mount in Venezuela after two huge earthquakes. SBS Correspondent Catalina Florez joins us from Caracas, where locals say the government is "nowhere to be seen". Plus, US and Iranian strikes resume, as a battle of wills over the Strait of Hormuz threatens to undo the ceasefire. Meanwhile, Cuba pushes through its biggest economic reforms since the communist revolution, and Batman Returns?! The caped crusader spotted in the most unlikely of places.

item.title: At least 13 killed after 'most massive' assault on Ukrainian capital since Russia's invasion
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Russia "has been preparing this massive strike against Ukraine for some time now".

item.title: Salah or the Socceroos? Australia's biggest Liverpool fans face World Cup dilemma
item.description: Ahead of Australia's clash with Egypt, one question dominates at Kleames Dawod's barbershop: can you cheer against Mohamed Salah?

item.title: World Cup: Kane rescues England; Belgium win on penalty; controversial red card for USA
item.description: Harry Kane gets England through to the FIFA World Cup 2026™ Round of 16, as they come from behind with a 2-1 win over DR Congo. Belgium pulls through with a controversial VAR penalty, and scores deep into stoppage time to beat Senegal 3-2. The United States have advanced to the World Cup Round of 16, after sealing a 2-nil victory against Bosnia and Herzegovina. Folarin Balogun scores the opening goal, just before half time, before being sent off with a red card in the second half. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she breaks down today's results, and looks into Socceroos' latest talking points ahead of their match against Egypt this Saturday.

item.title: Why super funds are looking overseas for strong returns as ETF listings hit a record
item.description: Australian shares ended the day little changed, but there is plenty happening beneath the surface. AMP Chief Investment Officer Anna Shelley explains why many superannuation funds delivered double-digit returns last financial year, the role global markets including South Korea played, and whether recent volatility could reshape investment strategies. Plus, Rory Cunningham from the ASX unpacks the record boom in exchange traded funds and why more Australians are investing through ETFs, before Chris Weston from Pepperstone wraps up the day's market action and the forces driving the Australian sharemarket.

item.title: A controversial opener, crack at fifth title and Aussies to watch: Your Tour de France guide
item.description: The world's biggest cycling spectacle, the Tour de France returns on 4 July. This year's race could be more challenging than ever.

item.title: Why are Australian landmarks turning red, white and blue for the US' 250th birthday?
item.description: Twenty-seven sites across four cities will be illuminated to mark America's 250th birthday — but not every state is taking part.

item.title: The Socceroos diehards putting on a festival in an unlikely location
item.description: The community of Jabal Mohsen has no football field of its own, but some here are going all out for the Socceroos.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia both vow to escalate conflict after Russian strikes on Kyiv kill 25
item.description: UN chief secretary-general António Guterres has condemned Russia's barrage and stressed the need for a ceasefire call.

item.title: WATCH: Switzerland reach last 16 for fourth straight World Cup, Ronaldo lifts Portugal
item.description: Spain went untroubled by Austria, who never quite managed to summon the momentum they needed.

item.title: Campaigners welcome UK government apology for historic role in forced adoptions
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has apologised to women forced to give up their babies or separated from their mothers at birth under a system of forced adoptions that targeted unmarried women for decades after World War II. An estimated 185,000 children were separated from their mothers in Britain.

item.title: What happens if Australia beat Egypt in the World Cup Round of 32?
item.description: A win over Egypt this weekend could mean the Socceroos face off against one of the best players in football history.

item.title: Eight arrested in UK in echoes of Pelicot case | Midday News Bulletin 3 July 2026
item.description: UK police arrest eight in global sexual abuse investigation; Labor criticises delay to social media reforms; and in the World Cup, Egypt's Mo Salah races to be fit for Socceroos showdown tomorrow.

item.title: Suspected bird flu case found in NSW, authorities say
item.description: This adds to the five known cases in two other states.

item.title: Nine monks die in Thailand accident caused by 11-year-old boy | Morning News Bulletin 3 July 2026
item.description: Australia's eSafety Commissioner appears at the antisemitism royal commission; an 11-year-old boy kills nine monks in Thailand; and in the World Cup, the Socceroos prepare for their must-win match against Egypt.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English | Online safety commissioner launches legal action against Elon Musk
item.description: A news bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australia facing unprecedented levels of online hate speech, antisemitism inquiry told
item.description: In its third week of public hearings, the antisemitism inquiry examined how online hate has manifested into antisemitic violence.

item.title: Inside the 'extremely complex' operation to save a Venezuelan trapped for eight days
item.description: On the ground in Venezuela, rescuers describe to SBS News the harrowing effort to retrieve a man who, against all odds, remained alive eight days after twin earthquakes rocked Venezuela.

item.title: Suspected case of bird flu hits New South Wales | Evening News Bulletin 3 July 2026
item.description: Bird flu fears hit New South Wales…a university chancellor quits ahead of ICAC questioning… and, at the the World Cup, Australia say they're prepared, whether Egyptian Mo Salah plays or not in the big Round of 32 match

item.title: 'A miracle': Man pulled from rubble in earthquake-hit Venezuela
item.description: A security guard has pulled out from under the rubble in Venezuela, eight days after twin earthquakes struck the country. Seven countries were involved in the rescue effort as part of what has been described as a complex logistical operation.

item.title: Fred started on scratchies aged 10. Later, he lost $225,000. He says Labor's leaving kids at risk
item.description: The government is attempting to pass its highly anticipated gambling ad reform bill against strong pushback from critics.

item.title: Record car sales as ASX rallies
item.description: Australians are buying new cars at a record pace despite ongoing cost of living pressures, with June sales reaching an all-time monthly high and electric vehicles and utes dominating the market.

item.title: More than 25,000 Australians are new millionaires. They're part of a growing global group
item.description: Swiss bank UBS has found that global wealth has expanded at its fastest pace since 2017. Here's why.

item.title: Your super may have been boosted by an unlikely sharemarket — here's how
item.description: An overseas stock exchange may have boosted your super last year. Plus, falling petrol prices and the rising ETF market.

item.title: 'I don't agree': One Nation supporters don't understand monoculturalism
item.description: This electorate "screams One Nation", but there's one issue supporters are confused about.

item.title: WATCH: Socceroos' World Cup dream shattered with 'cruel' shootout defeat to Egypt
item.description: The Socceroos got to their first ever World Cup penalty shootout. But painful misses cost them everything.

item.title: Free-range chicken owners advised to keep hens inside as bird flu threat spreads
item.description: Australia's animal health emergency body says poultry producers may want to keep free-range birds indoors for the next two weeks.

item.title: Socceroos heartbreak at the FIFA World Cup | Morning News Bulletin 4 July 2026
item.description: Egypt defeat the Socceroos on penalties to reach World Cup Round of 16; experts urge calm after suspected New South Wales bird flu detection; Ange Postecoglu appointed head coach of Saudi Pro League champions Al-Nassr.

item.title: Keiko Fujimori has won Peru's presidential race
item.description: Keiko Fujimori's win reaffirms Latin America's rightward shift, while her leftist opponent Roberto Sanchez says he won't recognise her government.

item.title: WATCH: Herrington reacts to 'speedbump' as football legend offers support for penalty miss
item.description: Defenders Harry Souttar and Lucas Herrington missed their penalties, but they didn't miss out on support afterwards.

item.title: 'Set the bar': Why the Socceroos' next World Cup campaign could go one better
item.description: The Socceroos fell short against Egypt, but their emerging stars suggest the best is still to come.

item.title: Socceroos' loss 'tough to take' | Midday Bulletin 4 July 2026
item.description: Penalty heartbreak ends Socceroos' World Cup dream; UNESCO backs Australia's Great Barrier Reef protections; Rugby League match ban for Cody Walker.

item.title: WATCH: Argentina, Colombia and Egypt into next round; Cape Verde, Australia and Ghana out
item.description: Colombia have sent Ghana packing in the final match of the World Cup Round of 32, with Argentina and Egypt also making it through.

item.title: Millions mourn as Iran begins week-long funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is not just the country's late Supreme Leader. He is now a martyr.

item.title: WATCH: Goalkeeper Patrick Beach reacts to controversial last-minute substitution
item.description: Beach said he "didn't know" he was to be subbed off before the shoot-out, which saw Australia knocked out of the World Cup.

item.title: Players and fans react to Socceroos elimination from World Cup | Evening News Bulletin 4 July 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, Australia says climate finance for Pacific nations is on the agenda ahead of the UN climate summit; a funeral held for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; and in football, players and fans react to the Socceroos elimination from the World Cup.

item.title: The World Cup Round of 16 is set. Here's every qualified team
item.description: The World Cup Round of 16 kicks off soon. Get ready for a series of exciting fixtures.

item.title: World Cup: Heartbreak for Socceroos in penalty shootout against Egypt, Argentina survive Cape Verde scare
item.description: The Socceroos' World Cup campaign ends in heartbreak at the FIFA World Cup 2026™ , as Egypt knocks them out of the match in penalties. Messi scores for Argentina but the world champions were given a World Cup scare by Cape Verde. Egypt advances in its first-ever knockout stage game at the World Cup, and will face Argentina in the Round of 16. Colombia beat Ghana 1-nil and are through to the Round of 16 against Switzerland. Join your host Haylena Krishnamoorthy for this episode of World Cup Daily | The 90+ Podcast, as she talks to former Socceroo and SBS pundit, Tommy Oar to break down Australia's results. How did Australia and Egypt's penalty shootout unfold? Was the Egyptian king, Mohamed Salah a World Cup threat to the Socceroos? What do Socceroos' fans have to say? How did Cape Verde stun Argentina?

item.title: Denis watched his ancestral lands be ravaged as a kid. It sparked a major change
item.description: Indigenous Protected Areas are a homegrown but relatively unheard-of conservation model, making waves in Australia and around the world.

item.title: The surprising factor shaping young Australians' family plans
item.description: New research finds most young Australians still want children, but a lack of trust in society is emerging as a surprising influence.

item.title: World Cup woes: How to help a child deal with distress about the Socceroos' exit
item.description: No parent likes seeing their child upset, but such moments can be opportunities to help strengthen children's emotional regulation.

item.title: Anthony Albanese embarks on Pacific tour | Morning News Bulletin 5 July 2026
item.description: Anthony Albanese begins a three-day trip to Fiji and the Solomon Islands; First Nations culture celebrated during NAIDOC Week; And in football, What's next for the Socceroos after the World Cup.

item.title: '50 Years of Deadly': What is NAIDOC Week, and what's behind this year's theme?
item.description: .National NAIDOC Week celebrations are being held across Australia this week to celebrate and recognise the history, culture and achievements of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. This year's theme is 'Fifty Years of Deadly', celebrating more than five decades of NAIDOC Week. NAIDOC Week runs from the 5th to the 12th of July. The Committee members say the milestone offers a chance to honour those who built the NAIDOC movement, and consider what still needs to change.

item.title: WATCH: Mbappé penalty lifts France past Paraguay to last eight, Morocco end Canada's dream
item.description: Now unbeaten in 34 matches, ‌Morocco will next play on 9 July against France.

item.title: One Nation came second in the Hunter electorate last election. What do voters there think now?
item.description: Support for One Nation has been on the rise in recent months. But in the electorate of Hunter - a Labor stronghold in New South Wales - the party was the runner up in last year's election, one of only two electorates where that happened. SBS News reporter Kerrin Thomas travelled to Hunter to speak to residents about how they're feeling about the party's latest moves.

item.title: Authorities confirm H5N1 strain found in NSW wild bird | Midday News Bulletin 5 July 2026
item.description: New South Wales escalates bird flu surveillance as testing confirms case of deadliest strain; The Prime Minister flies to Fiji, seeking a signature for another strategic treaty; And in the World Cup, France defeat Paraguay to reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: On fourth of July, an American pope sends a symbolic letter to the US about immigrants
item.description: The American pope spent 4 July praying for migrants at the epicentre of Europe's migration debate.

item.title: Why Egypt's coach flew the Palestinian flag after the Socceroos match
item.description: It isn't the first time the Palestinian flag has made an appearance inside a FIFA World Cup stadium.

item.title: Football Australia backs 'brave' Popovic amid criticism of Egypt calls
item.description: Football Australia's senior leadership defended Popovic, calling his World Cup run an "enormous success".
